turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: My phone keeps restarting by itself.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I see—that must be frustrating. How often does it restart during the day?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s an iPhone 13, about 2 years old.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thanks for confirming. Did this start after a recent update or installing a new app?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, it happened after the latest iOS update.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That helps. First, could you check if your storage is almost full? Low storage can cause restarts.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s nearly full. What should I do?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Try deleting unused apps or large videos, then restart the phone manually.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: How do I reset the settings? Will resetting delete my files or photos?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question. Resetting settings only won’t delete your data. Go to Settings > General > Transfer or Reset iPhone > Reset > Reset All Settings.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It restarted and seems more stable now.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a good sign. Please monitor it for the next few hours.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s working again! Appreciate your support.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome—glad to hear that.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: What if this problem keeps happening after today?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: If it continues, I recommend backing up your phone and visiting an Apple Store or authorized service provider for a hardware check.
